title: Philadelphus mandshuricus
taxonomy: Philadelphus mandshuricus is classified within the family Hydrangeaceae, commonly known as the hydrangea family. This classification places it among other flowering plants that share similarities in their growth habits and reproductive structures. The genus Philadelphus, to which Philadelphus mandshuricus belongs, contains around 50 species of flowering plants. uses: Philadelphus mandshuricus is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine. It is used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems.
